steps.action: Read frontend/src/main.tsx to inspect route definitions
steps.expected: A <Route> for path '/brainstorm' rendering BrainstormPage component exists within HashRouter
steps.observed: Line 7 imports BrainstormPage from './pages/BrainstormPage.tsx'. Both Electron and web route blocks contain <Route path="/brainstorm" element={<BrainstormPage />} />. HashRouter wraps all routes, so this renders at /#/brainstorm.

steps.action: Verify BrainstormPage.tsx file exists at import path
steps.observed: Glob confirmed file exists at frontend/src/pages/BrainstormPage.tsx

steps.action: Check catchall route does not shadow /brainstorm
steps.expected: The wildcard <Route path="*"> comes after /brainstorm, so /brainstorm matches first
steps.observed: In both Electron and web blocks, <Route path="/brainstorm" ... /> appears before <Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/" replace />} />

evidence.codeReview: main.tsx line 7: import BrainstormPage from './pages/BrainstormPage.tsx'; Electron block line 17: <Route path="/brainstorm" element={<BrainstormPage />} />; Web block line 23: <Route path="/brainstorm" element={<BrainstormPage />} />

assertions.title: Landing page has Brainstorm Mode navigation card

steps.action: Read frontend/src/pages/LandingPage.tsx and inspect indexCards array
steps.expected: An entry with key 'brainstorm' in the indexCards array with label 'Brainstorm Mode' targeting /#/brainstorm
steps.observed: indexCards array contains: { key: 'brainstorm', label: 'Brainstorm Mode', subtitle: 'Voice-driven ideation', icon: IconBrainstorm, color: 'amber' }

steps.expected: Clicking the brainstorm card sets window.location.hash to '#/brainstorm'
IconBrainstorm is imported from icons module

evidence.codeReview: LandingPage.tsx indexCards array includes { key: 'brainstorm', label: 'Brainstorm Mode', subtitle: 'Voice-driven ideation', icon: IconBrainstorm, color: 'amber' }. IndexView handleClick: window.location.hash = '#/brainstorm' for brainstorm card.

assertions.title: Route is accessible in both web and Electron modes

steps.action: Read main.tsx and inspect Electron conditional route block
steps.expected: The isElectron===true branch includes <Route path='/brainstorm' element={<BrainstormPage />} />
steps.observed: Lines 15-19 (Electron block): <Route path="/" element={<App />} />, <Route path="/brainstorm" element={<BrainstormPage />} />, <Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/" replace />} />

steps.action: Read main.tsx and inspect web (non-Electron) conditional route block
steps.expected: The isElectron===false branch includes <Route path='/brainstorm' element={<BrainstormPage />} />
steps.observed: Lines 21-25 (web block): <Route path="/" element={<LandingPage />} />, <Route path="/brainstorm" element={<BrainstormPage />} />, <Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/" replace />} />

steps.action: Confirm both blocks use the same BrainstormPage component
steps.expected: Both use <BrainstormPage /> from the single import
steps.observed: Both blocks reference the same BrainstormPage component imported at line 7

evidence.codeReview: main.tsx uses isElectron ternary to define two route blocks. Both blocks identically include: <Route path="/brainstorm" element={<BrainstormPage />} />. Electron detection: const isElectron = /electron/i.test(navigator.userAgent.toLowerCase()) || window.electronAPI !== undefined;

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed, 0 failed. All routing and navigation assertions for the brainstorm-frontend milestone are confirmed via code review. The /#/brainstorm route is properly registered in both Electron and web mode (FE-RN-01, FE-RN-03), and the LandingPage includes a Brainstorm Mode card navigating to it (FE-RN-02).
